summary: Iteration 5 backend testing — 17/17 PASS in 9.5s on /app/backend/tests/test_iter5_team_ratelimit.py. Verified ALL iter5 deliverables: (1) Team workspaces — GET /api/team/members returns members with admin shown is_owner=true; POST /api/team/invite creates teammate (workspace_id == owner.id confirmed in mongo), returns success+temp_password+user_id, teammate logs in successfully with temp_password; (2) Workspace data sharing — admin creates a lead, teammate's GET /api/leads sees the SAME lead by id (workspace-scoped via ws() helper); (3) Owner-only invite — non-owner POST /api/team/invite -> 403 'Only workspace owner can invite'; (4) Owner-only remove — teammate DELETE /api/team/members/{owner_id} -> 403, owner DELETE /api/team/members/{teammate_id} -> 200 success and user removed from db; (5) AI rate limit — seeded 60 ai_calls docs for a fresh invited user, 61st POST /api/seo/keywords returns 429 with 'AI rate limit reached' message; (6) SEO source field — POST /api/seo/keywords returns {source:'ai', keywords:[25 items]} when DataForSEO/SerpAPI not configured; (7) OAuth — GET /api/oauth/linkedin/start returns 400 with detail mentioning LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID, GET /api/oauth/foo/start returns 404 'Unknown provider'; (8) Inbox $lookup — seeded lead+inbound comm, GET /api/communications/inbox returns the message with hydrated lead:{name,email,phone}; (9) Briefing prefs — POST /api/briefing/preferences {daily_email:true, hour_utc:8} -> {success:true}, GET returns same; flipped to {false, 13} -> persisted; (10) Regression — admin login admin/admin123 OK, dashboard/stats OK, leads CRUD (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) OK, integrations GET OK, SSRF block on auto-fill (169.254.169.254) -> 400. Test data isolated with TEST_iter5_<run_id> prefix and cleaned up in teardown.

minor.endpoint: PRE-EXISTING (not iter5 regression) — multiple AI endpoints (/api/market/analyze, /api/seo/backlinks, /api/seo/content-gaps, /api/pr/media-list, /api/growth-plan/generate, /api/briefing/generate, /api/leads/score-batch)
minor.issue: When Groq's upstream tokens-per-day quota is exhausted (saw 'Rate limit reached on tokens per day (TPD): Limit 100000, Used 99420' in backend.err.log), _groq_chat raises and the endpoints return 502 'AI provider returned invalid output. Please retry.' Same as iter3/iter4 502 fallback path — but the user-facing message says 'invalid output' which is misleading when the cause is upstream TPD/RPM rate limit. Suggest distinguishing groq.RateLimitError -> 429 with 'AI provider rate limit, retry later' from json_validate_failed -> 502 'invalid output'.

Returns the temp_password in the JSON response body — convenient for testing but a minor secret-leak concern in prod (the password is also emailed). Consider gating temp_password in response behind a debug flag, or returning it only when SMTP fails.

Removes the user document but does NOT cascade or reassign their owned leads/campaigns/communications. Since data is workspace-scoped (user_id == workspace_id == owner.id), nothing is orphaned for owner-created data, but any leads/campaigns the teammate explicitly created with their own user.id (if such a path exists in future) would be orphaned. Currently safe because every write uses ws(user). Document this invariant or add an explicit assertion.
On token exchange failure, raises HTTPException(400) with raw exception string in detail — could leak provider error internals. Prefer a sanitized message + log the full error server-side.

action_items: Optional: distinguish groq.RateLimitError (TPD/RPM) -> 429 retry-later vs json_validate_failed -> 502 in _groq_chat error handling so users see the right message.
Optional: hide temp_password from /api/team/invite response body (only email it).
Optional: split server.py (now 2240 lines) into routers/{auth,leads,team,ai,oauth,growth_studio,webhooks,scheduler}.py — flagged in iter4, not addressed.
No retest required for iter5 features.
critical_code_review_comments: ws(user) helper at server.py:83 cleanly returns user.workspace_id||user.id — every data query has been migrated (53 occurrences confirmed). Migration in startup backfills workspace_id=user_id for existing users so admin's existing data remains visible. Solid pattern.

check_ai_rate_limit() at server.py:1828 uses count_documents on db.ai_calls with ts > cutoff — correct sliding window. NOTE: the count is keyed on user_id (not workspace_id), so each teammate has their own 60/hr quota; the workspace_id field is logged but unused for limit enforcement. This is fine but worth documenting (per-user, not per-workspace).
Owner check at /api/team/invite and /api/team/members/{id} DELETE uses `user["id"] != ws(user)` — correct because owner's id == workspace_id by construction. Verified end-to-end.
OAuth state is persisted with TTL-less collection oauth_states — consider TTL index (e.g. expireAfterSeconds=600) so stale/abandoned states self-clean, otherwise the collection grows unbounded.

_real_keyword_data() is well-structured (DataForSEO -> SerpAPI -> None fallback). _opp_score formula is naive ((volume/100) - difficulty + 60 clamped 0..100); it works as a heuristic but should be documented.
server.py is now 2240 lines (was 1785 in iter4) — long overdue for splitting into routers/. Recommend doing this in iter6 before adding more features.

APScheduler is started in @app.on_event('startup'). With multiple uvicorn workers this would run the briefing/IMAP jobs N times. Currently only 1 worker (supervisor) so OK, but a leader-election or 'run-only-on-worker-0' guard would be wise for production.
Inbox $lookup pipeline is correct (matches by id, projects only safe fields, excludes _id) — addresses the iter4 N+1 concern. Good fix.
test_credentials: admin@zeromark.ai / admin123 (auto-seeded). Runtime test users TEST_iter5_mate_<run>, TEST_iter5_kick_<run>, TEST_iter5_rl_<run> created/cleaned per run.
seed_data_creation: All test artefacts use TEST_iter5_<run_id> prefix and are removed in finally blocks / fixture teardown — verified mongo state clean post-run (only admin user + admin's pre-existing data remain).
context_for_next_testing_agent: PUBLIC URL is https://instant-ship-2.preview.emergentagent.com (frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). The .env in backend has a different PUBLIC_APP_URL pointing to eda3142b… — that one is wrong/stale and is used by OAuth redirect_uri + invite emails. Worth flagging to main agent if OAuth callbacks need to actually work. /app/backend/tests/test_iter5_team_ratelimit.py is the iter5 regression suite; keep it green. NOTE: iter4 regression suite (test_iter4_reliability.py) shows 9 failures when run today, all due to Groq UPSTREAM tokens-per-day quota exhaustion (99420/100000) — not a code regression. Wait for the daily reset or upgrade Groq plan; the code path is correct.
rca of the issue: No new bugs in iter5. All 11 reviewer-listed items verified working. Minor pre-existing issue: when Groq returns 429 (TPD/RPM) instead of json_validate_failed, the user-facing 502 message says 'invalid output' — misleading but cosmetic. Workspace data-sharing root mechanism: ws(user) returns user.workspace_id (or user.id fallback for solo). All data writes/reads now use ws(user) instead of user[id], so all teammates with workspace_id == owner.id share the owner's data. Migration on startup ensures existing users (incl admin) have workspace_id=user_id, so no data is lost.
